parameterGroups.label: Send Routing
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls which Send Container receives the signal and at which channel offset

parameters.name: Send Index
parameters.description: Index of the send container to receive the routed signal

parameters.name: Channel Offset
parameters.description: Channel offset applied when routing into the send container

parameterGroups.label: Send Level
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls the gain level and smoothing of the send signal

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: Send gain in decibels applied before routing to the send container

parameters.name: Smoothing
parameters.description: Enables smoothing of gain changes to reduce clicks

standaloneModChains.name: Send Modulation
standaloneModChains.note: Modulates the send gain. Applied per-block as start/end values for ramped gain.

gaps.question: How does the SendEffect route audio to the SendContainer? Does it pass through audio or does it only copy/send?
gaps.context: Need to clarify whether the original signal is preserved (send) or diverted (insert).

gaps.question: How does the Smoothing toggle affect gain changes? What is the smoothing time? Is it per-sample or per-block?
gaps.context: Users need to know when to enable/disable smoothing for automation scenarios.

gaps.question: How does SendIndex connect to a specific SendContainer? Is it by creation order?
gaps.context: Users need to understand how to target a specific container.

gaps.question: What happens when the SendEffect is bypassed? Does the send signal ramp to zero or cut immediately?
gaps.context: Important for live automation and avoiding clicks.

gaps.question: How is the Send Modulation chain applied? Per-sample or per-block? How does it interact with the gain parameter?
gaps.context: Users need to know the modulation resolution for the send level.

notes: SendFX is the send side of the send/return pair with SendContainer. The default gain is -100 dB (silent), so the user must raise it to hear any send signal. The Smoothing parameter defaults to On. The Send Modulation chain has no parameterIndex - it's a standalone modulation applied to the send gain.
